documentTitle: Application for Authorized Care Order — Fictional Case A

cards.text: The evaluated person presents severe psychomotor agitation incompatible with a return to an ordinary living environment.
According to the psychiatric report of January 14th, the person refused all medication over the past six months.
cards.humanNote: To verify: the January 14th report is not attached to the file.

cards.text: The aggressive behavior observed during the 2019 hospitalization is documented as a determining antecedent.
The family reports a marked deterioration over the past two months.
The person denies all symptoms and claims to be in good health. This denial is interpreted as a sign of lack of insight.
The evaluator states that the person represents a danger to herself without citing any recent specific incident.
The 2017 diagnosis of paranoid schizophrenia is reproduced without documented re-evaluation.
The person maintained part-time employment for three years — a fact not mentioned in the evaluation.
cards.humanNote: Confirmed by employment records — absent from the report.

cards.text: The 2021 order noted a favorable prognosis contingent on outpatient follow-up. This prognosis is not referenced in the current application.
The requesting physician declares that all alternatives to compulsory care have been exhausted.
cards.humanNote: No alternative outpatient plan is described anywhere in the file.

cards.text: A crisis episode from 2019 is described as 'recent' in an application filed in 2024.
The person attended group therapy sessions in 2022 and 2023 according to community health center records — absent from the application.
The evaluator cites a fifteen-minute interview as the basis for a complete clinical assessment.
cards.humanNote: Interview duration confirmed in nursing notes.

cards.text: The report refers to a 'long history of non-compliance' without specifying its nature or duration.
The person allegedly stated a desire to stop all medication, according to an undated note from a social worker.
cards.humanNote: Undated document filed without author identification.

cards.text: The prognosis without treatment is described as 'bleak' without reference to clinical literature or comparable cases.
A 2018 social worker report — predating the initial 2021 application — is cited as a current clinical reference.
The person explicitly refused to sign the consent form, which is documented but not discussed in the analysis.
The physician concludes that the person is incapable of understanding the nature of her condition, without a documented cognitive assessment.
cards.humanNote: No neuropsychological evaluation or capacity assessment in the file.

cards.text: The application requests a 36-month care authorization — above common practice — without specific justification.
